source_draft: Sierra Vista Wave-A draft (superseded; audit trail in fixtures/realistic-mixed.SOURCE_NOTES.md)
description: Realistic-mixed profile: five plausible-typo violations planted deep in the arguments of the Sierra Vista demurrer, the kind a proofreader's eye might glaze past on a late-night read-through.

violations.snippet_after: ntative.  (Pub. Cont. Code, section 20118.4, subd. (a).)  Califor
violations.description: Inside a parenthetical code citation, `§` was replaced with the word `section`. LS Manual requires `§` (section symbol) inside parens; `section` spelled out only in running prose outside parens.
violations.verification_hint: Body paragraph 60 (the Pub. Cont. Code § 20118.4 discussion) contains `(Pub. Cont. Code, section 20118.4, subd. (a).)` — the only occurrence of the word `section` inside parens in the document. Grep for `, section ` preceded by an opening paren, or scan for `section` tokens whose nearest preceding parenthesis is `(`.

violations.snippet_before: ey (1915) 171 Cal. 150, 154.)  Contractors who deal with
violations.snippet_after: ey (1915) 171 Cal. 150, 154.) Contractors who deal with
violations.description: Single space between a citation sentence and the following body sentence (should be two spaces per LS Manual). Mid-paragraph in the account-stated argument — the classic transition where a proofreader's eye slides past.
violations.verification_hint: In body paragraph 62, search for `171 Cal. 150, 154.) Contractors` — exactly one space between `)` and `Contractors`. All other sentence terminators in that paragraph still carry two spaces.

violations.description: Single space between two body sentences (`17605. Pre-bid representations`) where the clean draft had two. Mid-paragraph in the declaratory-relief argument.
violations.verification_hint: In body paragraph 72, search for `17605. Pre-bid` — exactly one space between the period and the capital P. All other sentence terminators in that paragraph still carry two spaces.

violations.snippet_before: tions can bind the District despite the absence of
violations.snippet_after: tions can bind the district despite the absence of
violations.description: Lowercase `district` where the reference is unambiguously to the defined party-substitute (`the District` = Sierra Vista Unified School District). Deep in the equitable-estoppel argument, parallel to an earlier `the District` in the same sentence that was left capitalized — an inconsistency typical of a proofreader's miss.
violations.verification_hint: Body paragraph 81 contains exactly one lowercase `the district` reference (`bind the district despite`); the same paragraph also contains two properly capitalized `the District` references as controls. A rule-checker scanning for defined-term `District` should flag only this one token as inconsistent.

violations.description: Transposed digits in the first-page citation for *Air Quality Products, Inc. v. State of California* (1979): real cite is `96 Cal.App.3d 340`; corrupt cite is `96 Cal.App.3d 304`. The transposition is subtle (swap of last two digits), looks like a typo, but CourtListener lookup on `96 Cal.App.3d 304` does not return the Air Quality Products case.
violations.verification_hint: In body paragraph 82, the *Air Quality Products* cite reads `96 Cal.App.3d 304, 348–349`. Seed list entry is `Air Quality Products, Inc. v. State of California (1979) 96 Cal.App.3d 340`. A citation hallucination checker looking up the corrupt first-page against CourtListener should fail to match the case name.
